questions.q: By what title is Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel popularly known?
questions.explanation: He is popularly known as the Iron Man of India.

questions.q: Which office did Sardar Patel hold as independent India's first?
questions.explanation: He was independent India's first Deputy Prime Minister.

questions.q: Which cabinet portfolio did Sardar Patel hold in independent India's first government?
questions.explanation: He served as the first Home Minister of independent India.

questions.q: Sardar Patel is chiefly credited with which national achievement?
questions.explanation: He led the integration of over 500 princely states into the Indian Union.

questions.q: Approximately how many princely states did Sardar Patel help integrate into the Union?
questions.explanation: He led the integration of over 500 princely states.

questions.q: On which date is National Unity Day observed in honour of Sardar Patel?
questions.explanation: His birthday, 31 October, is observed as National Unity Day.

questions.q: Which observance marks Sardar Patel's birthday?
questions.explanation: His birthday is observed as National Unity Day (Rashtriya Ekta Diwas).

questions.q: What was the rank of Sardar Patel as a minister relative to the Prime Minister in the first cabinet?
questions.explanation: He was the first Deputy Prime Minister of India.

questions.q: The honorific 'Sardar' associated with Vallabhbhai Patel means roughly which of the following?
questions.explanation: The title 'Sardar' denotes a chief or leader.

questions.q: Sardar Patel served in the government of which Prime Minister?
questions.explanation: He served as Deputy Prime Minister and Home Minister under Prime Minister Jawaharlal Nehru.

questions.q: In which year did Sardar Patel begin serving as Deputy Prime Minister and Home Minister?
questions.explanation: He took office in 1947 when India became independent.

questions.q: Which ministry, under Patel, coordinated the accession of the princely states?
questions.explanation: As Home Minister he oversaw the States Ministry, which handled the princely states' integration.

questions.q: The colossal statue commemorating Sardar Patel is known by which name?
questions.explanation: The Statue of Unity in Gujarat commemorates Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel.

questions.q: In which state is the Statue of Unity dedicated to Sardar Patel located?
questions.explanation: The Statue of Unity is located in Gujarat.

questions.q: Sardar Patel chaired which committee of the Constituent Assembly dealing with citizen guarantees?
options: The Advisory Committee on Fundamental Rights
questions.explanation: He chaired the Advisory Committee on Fundamental Rights and Minorities in the Constituent Assembly.

questions.q: Sardar Patel was a senior leader of which national movement organisation before independence?
questions.explanation: He was a leading figure in the Indian National Congress during the freedom movement.

questions.q: The integration of princely states led by Patel was largely completed by which period?
questions.explanation: Most princely states acceded to India around 1947 to 1949 under his leadership.

questions.q: Sardar Patel's role as Home Minister made him responsible for which broad area?
questions.explanation: As Home Minister he was responsible for internal affairs, including relations with the states.

questions.q: In which year did Sardar Patel pass away while still in office?
questions.explanation: He died in 1950, while serving as Deputy Prime Minister and Home Minister.

questions.q: Sardar Patel is regarded as a patron of the All India Services, which form the backbone of which system?
questions.explanation: He is regarded as a patron of the All India Services that staff the civil administration.

questions.q: Which observance was instituted in 2014 to mark Sardar Patel's birth anniversary?
questions.explanation: Rashtriya Ekta Diwas (National Unity Day) is observed on his birthday, 31 October.

questions.q: In which year was Sardar Patel born?
questions.explanation: Sardar Vallabhbhai Patel was born in 1875.

questions.q: In which year was Sardar Patel posthumously conferred the Bharat Ratna?
questions.explanation: He was awarded the Bharat Ratna posthumously in 1991.

questions.q: The instrument through which princely states acceded to the Union under Patel's stewardship was called what?
questions.explanation: Princely states joined India by signing the Instrument of Accession.

questions.q: Which senior civil servant worked closely with Sardar Patel as Secretary of the States Ministry on integration?
questions.explanation: V.P. Menon worked with Patel as the key civil servant in the States Ministry.

questions.q: Sardar Patel earned the title 'Sardar' for his leadership of which 1928 agrarian satyagraha?
questions.explanation: His leadership of the 1928 Bardoli Satyagraha earned him the title 'Sardar'.

questions.q: Sardar Patel served as President of the Indian National Congress at which session?
questions.explanation: He presided over the Congress at its 1931 Karachi session.

questions.q: The committee chaired by Patel in the Constituent Assembly dealt with fundamental rights and which related subject?
questions.explanation: He chaired the Advisory Committee on Fundamental Rights, Minorities and Tribal and Excluded Areas.

questions.q: In which Gujarat city was Sardar Patel's early legal and political base established?
questions.explanation: He built his early legal practice and political base in Ahmedabad, Gujarat.

questions.q: Besides Home Affairs, which ministry did Sardar Patel head that handled relations with the princely states?
questions.explanation: Patel headed the Ministry of States, which coordinated the integration of the princely states, alongside Home Affairs.
